interactions.summary: Jiang traces Napoleon's mythic career choices: Italy for Caesar, Egypt for Alexander and Caesar, the coup because other republican generals refuse, and Marengo as action staged for legend.

He contrasts messianic selflessness with Napoleon making himself emperor, distributing Europe to family, and revealing obsessive selfishness through letters to Josephine and the line that power is his mistress.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang with quoted Napoleon letters in seg-0047 and seg-0048.

speaker_notes.note: Jiang reads Napoleon's letters to Josephine; quoted insults are Napoleon's words as presented in class.

claims.claim: Napoleon chose Italy and Egypt because those theaters let him act out Caesar, Hannibal, and Alexander myths.
Napoleon succeeded in the 1799 coup because republican generals refused while he wanted to become the Caesar who ends the republic.
Napoleon repeatedly took stupid military risks because action mattered more than reality for sustaining his myth.
claims.temporal_scope: Lecture diagnosis of Marengo/Italian campaign myth-making.

claims.claim: Napoleon destroyed the revolution by making himself emperor and turning Europe into family property.
Napoleon's need for war came from the need to maintain his messianic mythology and thereby control the French people.

signature_moments.moment: Napoleon wants to be the new Caesar crossing the Rubicon and ending the republic.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the new Caesar who crushes the Rubicon and ends the Roman Republic
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It makes coup politics legible as theatrical role-play.

signature_moments.moment: Napoleon does not need the battle to be real in the right way; he needs action that can be converted into legend.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I need action in order to create my mythology
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the mechanism of myth-making stripped down to performance.

signature_moments.moment: Power is not merely an instrument for Napoleon; power is his mistress.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The quoted line crystallizes Jiang's narcissism diagnosis.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The ability to stage action and perception so people see the actor as a messianic historical figure.
The role Napoleon performs to keep revolutionary people attached to him; Jiang contrasts it with Robespierre's selfless version.

chronology_notes.note: The lecture identifies 1799 as the coup moment when Napoleon helps end the Republic.
The lecture identifies 1804 as Napoleon making himself emperor and thereby destroying the revolution.
